Summary: Draft outreach messaging for partner recruitment
Description:
Issue hierarchy/context: This Task belongs to parent Story `TGTM-89`: Build property management partner shortlist. Grandparent context: `TGTM-8`: Recruit legal, valuation, audit, KYC, custody, payments, and servicing partners. It should be executed as part of the `Property management and servicing partner recruitment` workstream and should produce a concrete artifact that supports the parent Story outcome.

Original intent: Prepare introductory messaging and a partnership pitch to engage shortlisted property management and servicing providers.

How we are doing this: Use the parent Story objective as the frame, then complete this task as a specific execution step. For this workstream, the broader objective is to identify property management and ongoing asset servicing partners that can support tenant placement, rent collection support, maintenance coordination, inspections, owner reporting, and investor-grade property performance operations.

Preliminary solution: Prepare partner outreach assets for Property management and servicing partner recruitment: founder intro email, LinkedIn note, short pitch paragraph, qualification questions, partner value proposition, and meeting agenda. The message should explain Blocks Estate, why this partner is relevant, what collaboration is being explored, and what information is needed next.

Expected deliverable: Partner outreach brief with email copy, LinkedIn copy, call agenda, qualification questions, and follow-up template.

Inputs / data required: Dubai property management firms, developer referrals, service menus, pricing, sample owner reports, tenant placement records, maintenance SLAs, inspection processes, licensing evidence, and client references.

Evaluation / decision criteria: service coverage, Dubai/UAE property operations experience, reporting quality, responsiveness, maintenance network, rent collection process, technology maturity, scalability, pricing, references, and fit with investor reporting needs.

Primary stakeholders: operations, investor servicing, developer acquisition, property underwriting, finance, customer success.

Implementation notes:
- Capture evidence, not only opinions, for every candidate or recommendation.
- Use consistent fields so this task can roll up into the parent Story tracker.
- Flag assumptions, unavailable information, and recommended follow-up questions instead of hiding uncertainty.

Acceptance criteria:
- The output artifact is complete, structured, and stored where the GTM team can access it.
- Relevant candidates, criteria, or evidence are documented with sources or clear assumptions.
- The work product links back to the parent Story objective and supports a partner decision or next action.
- Risks, dependencies, open questions, and recommended next steps are explicitly captured.

Summary: Recruit legal, valuation, audit, KYC, custody, payments, and servicing partners
Description:
Issue hierarchy/context: This is the Partnerships domain Epic for the GTM program. It contains 8 visible child Stories in this extract and coordinates partner recruitment across legal, valuation, audit/review, KYC/AML, custody/wallet, payment/banking, property management, credibility partners, and enterprise pilot candidates.

Original intent: Assemble the trust partner ecosystem needed for credible issuance, onboarding, custody, AED payment flows, valuation, audit/review reports, and property servicing.

How we are doing this: Treat partnerships as a launch-readiness system rather than a logo-collection exercise. Each partner category should move through the same operating rhythm: define the required capability, build a longlist, segment candidates, identify decision makers, run outreach, qualify through a standard scorecard, conduct diligence/reference checks, compare commercial and implementation trade-offs, then recommend preferred and backup partners.

Preliminary solution: Build a central Partnerships Partner Operating Model with one master tracker, one weighted scorecard, one outreach library, one diligence checklist, and one decision memo format. The master tracker should show category, candidate, strategic fit, service coverage, UAE/Dubai relevance, implementation readiness, commercial terms, risk, trust value, current stage, owner, next step, and target decision date.

Expected deliverable: A complete partnerships operating plan with partner categories, shortlists, evaluation logic, outreach workflow, partner commitments, and launch-readiness governance.

Inputs / data required: Dubai/UAE law firm directories, DIFC/ADGM practitioner lists, referrals from developers and family offices, prior tokenization/fintech mandates, real estate transaction credentials, partner bios, fee models, and responsiveness from discovery outreach.

Evaluation / decision criteria: jurisdiction coverage, tokenization/regulatory experience, real estate depth, developer credibility, ability to produce investor-grade documentation, partner availability, commercial flexibility, response speed, references, and conflicts.

Primary stakeholders: founders, compliance lead, operations lead, product/legal documentation owner, developer acquisition team, investor trust/compliance team.

Implementation notes:
- Use the same evaluation language across all partner categories so leadership can compare legal, valuation, audit, KYC/AML, custody, banking, and servicing decisions consistently.
- Separate strategic credibility value from operational readiness; a partner can be useful for trust-building but still not ready for live launch operations.
- Maintain preferred, backup, and future-state options so the GTM launch is not blocked by one vendor or partner.

Acceptance criteria:
- All visible child Stories have expanded execution detail and clear partner-selection outcomes.
- Each partner category has a defined evaluation method, preliminary solution, and deliverable standard.
- Partner dependencies are clear enough for founders, GTM, compliance, product, and operations teams to execute without reinterpreting the strategy.
- At least two priority credibility partner paths are identifiable from the combined workstream outputs.
